key_insight: MALOQ is a scalable machine-learning framework that predicts quantum-transport Hamiltonian/density operators for systems ranging from a few atoms to 100,000 atoms using an SO(2)-equivariant model and distributed graph processing.
engineering_relevance: It enables much faster electronic-structure modeling for large, chemically diverse materials, making DFT-like quantum simulations more practical on modern supercomputers.
method: Custom data-processing kernels for high-rank Hamiltonian matrix data
Edge-wise distributed atomic graph handling for scalable training and inference
evidence_signal: The authors report training on the largest available molecular Hamiltonian datasets and show over 30% lower time-per-epoch than molecule-wise distribution, with scalable runs on the Alps supercomputer for 3,000–12,000 atoms using up to 192 GPUs for training and 256 GPUs for inference.
watch_item: Performance depends on specialized distributed data handling and large-scale GPU infrastructure, so portability and efficiency may vary outside the tested supercomputer environment.
paper_title: MALOQ: Massively Accelerated Learning of Operators for Quantum Transport
